title: Historical - Export controls on Computers have been around a long time
text:
Xebec
Well-known member

A little OT, but given all of the discussion over the last couple of years on the controls around exporting AI, GPUs, and what that means for the industry.. It's worth remembering this is hardly a new thing:

September 24, 1999

Paris, France — Apple Computer is trying to persuade the U.S. government to lift restrictions on exports of the new PowerPC G4 processor-based Power Mac systems, the acting head of the company said at Apple Expo on Wednesday.

“The Power Mac G4 is so fast that it is classified as a supercomputer by the U.S. government, and we are prohibited from exporting it to over 50 nations worldwide,” said Steve Jobs, Apple’s interim chief executive officer.

Apple Tries to Get G4 Export Ban Lifted - HPCwire

NEWS BRIEFS Paris, France — Apple Computer is trying to persuade the U.S. government to lift restrictions on exports of the new PowerPC G4 processor-based Power Mac systems, the acting head of the company said at Apple Expo on Wednesday. “The Power Mac G4 is so fast that it is classified as a...

www.hpcwire.com

(Forgive the Apple marketing speak, but who knows what would happen if 500 MHz processors fell into the hands of our adversaries?? ).
